better to build this as an app with clickup integration?

• Yes. Build it as a lightweight app, with ClickUp as the execution layer.
  The right split:
  App = intelligence system
  It owns the source database, web listeners, search queries, dedupe, scoring, organization watchlists, country coverage,
  event tracking, source freshness, and opportunity history.
  ClickUp = operating workflow
  It owns tasks, owners, status, due dates, daily execution, outreach follow-up, collateral requests, meetings, and team
  accountability.
  Do not try to make ClickUp the whole database. It will get messy fast. ClickUp is great for pipeline execution, but weak for
  a forensic source graph.
  Best MVP
  Build a Fundraising Intelligence app with:
  1. Sources database
     DFIs, VCs, accelerators, events, foundations, trade bodies, ministries, banks, buyers, refineries, commodity orgs.
  2. Opportunity inbox
     Every signal from search, RSS, page watch, Gmail, manual LinkedIn capture.
  3. Organization graph
     Funders, investors, banks, buyers, regulators, accelerators, events, with country/theme/product fit.
  4. Scoring engine
     Africa fit, product match, urgency, warm intro, ticket size, strategic value.
  5. ClickUp sync
     Push only qualified records into ClickUp as tasks. Pull back status, owner, next action, close reason.
  6. Daily digest
     “New P1 opportunities, deadlines, stale leads, source changes, hot intros.”
  7. Country map
     54-country coverage: regulators, ministries, procurement portals, startup resources, banks, commodity bodies.
  Open/free stack
  Use:
  - Postgres for the source/opportunity database
  - Next.js or simple internal web app
  - n8n for listeners and automation
  - changedetection.io for page monitoring
  - RSS/API/email alerts where available
  - Meilisearch or Postgres full-text search
  - ClickUp API for task sync
  - Gmail/Calendar integration for comms/meetings
  Important design rule
  LinkedIn should remain manual or semi-manual capture unless you have approved API access. The app can store LinkedIn URLs,
  notes, excerpts, people, orgs, and warm-intro paths, but should not scrape LinkedIn feeds automatically.
  I’d build this next as:
  fundraising/app/
  - sources
  - organizations
  - opportunities
  - signals
  - queries
  - events
  - clickup_sync
  Then connect it to the source files we already created. This gives GTCX a real fundraising intelligence engine, not just a
  ClickUp board.

     12 +# Fundraising Agent Email Operations
     13 +
     14 +This contract defines the mailbox, aliases, labels, permissions, and guardrails
     15 +for an agent-assisted fundraising inbox.
     16 +
     17 +## Recommended Mailbox
     18 +
     19 +Create one role mailbox:
     20 +
     21 +```text
     22 +fundraising.ops@gtcx.africa
     23 +```
     24 +
     25 +Display name:
     26 +
     27 +```text
     28 +GTCX Fundraising Operations
     29 +```
     30 +
     31 +Use this as the agent-operated fundraising inbox. Do not use a founder's
     32 +personal mailbox as the automation surface.
     33 +
     34 +## Public Aliases
     35 +
     36 +Attach aliases or Google Groups that forward into the role mailbox:
     37 +
     38 +| Address | Use |
     39 +| ------- | --- |
     40 +| `fundraising@gtcx.africa` | General capital, grants, sponsorship, challenge, and investor communication |
     41 +| `investors@gtcx.africa` | VC, angel, family office, catalytic capital |
     42 +| `partnerships@gtcx.africa` | DFIs, banks, trade bodies, governments, ecosystem partners |
     43 +| `pilots@gtcx.africa` | Paid pilots, design partners, public-sector pilots |
     44 +| `applications@gtcx.africa` | Accelerator, challenge, grant, and program applications |
     45 +| `accounts@gtcx.africa` | Account registrations, portals, tools, startup credits |
     46 +
     47 +Use plus-addressing for individual platforms when accepted:
     48 +
     49 +```text
     50 +accounts+africarena@gtcx.africa
     51 +accounts+gitex@gtcx.africa
     52 +applications+abh@gtcx.africa
     53 +applications+gsma@gtcx.africa
     54 +```
     55 +
     56 +## Account Creation Rules
     57 +
     58 +The agent may prepare account registrations, but account ownership stays with
     59 +GTCX.
     60 +
     61 +| Rule | Requirement |
     62 +| ---- | ----------- |
     63 +| Legal owner | GTCX entity or named human admin, not the agent |
     64 +| Email | Use `accounts@gtcx.africa` or plus-addressed alias |
     65 +| Passwords | Store in shared password manager only; never in ClickUp, Gmail, docs, or chat |
     66 +| MFA | Use authenticator app/security key owned by human admin or approved vault workflow |
     67 +| Recovery | Recovery email and phone must be human-controlled |
     68 +| Paid services | Human approval before entering cards, billing, paid tiers, or trials that auto-renew |
     69 +| Terms | Human approval before accepting terms with legal, financial, IP, data, or exclusivity implications |
     70 +
     71 +## Gmail Labels
     72 +
     73 +Create these labels in the role mailbox:
     74 +
     75 +| Label | Purpose |
     76 +| ----- | ------- |
     77 +| `fundraising/inbox` | Unprocessed fundraising email |
     78 +| `fundraising/investors` | Investors, angels, family offices, funds |
     79 +| `fundraising/partners` | DFIs, banks, governments, trade bodies, ecosystem partners |
     80 +| `fundraising/pilots` | Paid pilots, design partners, revenue opportunities |
     81 +| `fundraising/applications` | Accelerators, challenges, grants, program submissions |
     82 +| `fundraising/accounts` | Platform signups, verification links, portal access |
     83 +| `fundraising/action-needed` | Human decision required |
     84 +| `fundraising/draft-ready` | Agent draft ready for review |
     85 +| `fundraising/sent` | Outreach sent |
     86 +| `fundraising/replied` | External reply received |
     87 +| `fundraising/archive` | Processed and archived |
     88 +
     89 +## ClickUp Mapping
     90 +
     91 +Every actionable email should map to a ClickUp task in `Fundraising Opportunities`.
     92 +
     93 +| Gmail Signal | ClickUp Action |
     94 +| ------------ | -------------- |
     95 +| New investor/program/partner email | Create or update opportunity task |
     96 +| Account verification email | Update account/setup task |
     97 +| Positive reply | Move task to `Meeting Scheduled` or `Diligence` |
     98 +| Application confirmation | Link confirmation to opportunity |
     99 +| Deadline reminder | Update deadline and daily digest |
    100 +| Rejection/no fit | Move to `Lost / Parked` with reason |
    101 +
    102 +Store only summaries and links in ClickUp. Do not copy full private email bodies
    103 +unless explicitly needed and approved.
    104 +
    105 +## Agent Permissions
    106 +
    107 +Start with human-approved drafting only.
    108 +
    109 +| Capability | Phase 1 | Phase 2 | Phase 3 |
    110 +| ---------- | ------- | ------- | ------- |
    111 +| Read labeled fundraising inbox | Yes | Yes | Yes |
    112 +| Create ClickUp tasks | Yes | Yes | Yes |
    113 +| Draft replies | Yes | Yes | Yes |
    114 +| Send emails | No | Human-approved only | Limited approved templates |
    115 +| Create portal accounts | Prepare only | Human-approved | Human-approved |
    116 +| Accept terms/contracts | No | No | No |
    117 +| Enter payment info | No | No | No |
    118 +| Delete emails | No | No | No |
    119 +
    120 +## n8n Workflows
    121 +
    122 +| Workflow | Trigger | Output |
    123 +| -------- | ------- | ------ |
    124 +| Gmail to ClickUp | New email with fundraising label | Create/update opportunity task |
    125 +| Account verification tracker | New email with `fundraising/accounts` | Update account setup task |
    126 +| Draft response | Task moves to `Outreach Ready` or reply arrives | Gmail draft + ClickUp comment |
    127 +| Application deadline watcher | Email or task deadline | Daily digest entry |
    128 +| Reply classifier | Reply on tracked thread | Stage update and follow-up task |
    129 +
    130 +## Human Approval Gates
    131 +
    132 +Human approval is required before:
    133 +
    134 +1. Sending external email from GTCX.
    135 +2. Submitting an application.
    136 +3. Accepting terms.
    137 +4. Creating accounts that bind GTCX to legal, financial, data, IP, or exclusivity terms.
    138 +5. Entering billing details.
    139 +6. Sharing confidential docs, decks, financials, source code, or data rooms.
    140 +7. Scheduling meetings with government, regulators, banks, DFIs, investors, or buyers.
    141 +
    142 +## Daily Agent Routine
    143 +
    144 +Each weekday:
    145 +
    146 +1. Review new role-mailbox messages.
    147 +2. Label and summarize each relevant thread.
    148 +3. Create/update ClickUp tasks.
    149 +4. Draft replies for human review.
    150 +5. Update application/account setup trackers.
    151 +6. Add hot replies and deadlines to daily digest.
    152 +7. Park spam/no-fit messages.
    153 +
    154 +## Security Baseline
    155 +
    156 +- Enable 2FA on the mailbox.
    157 +- Restrict mailbox access to named admins and approved automation credentials.
    158 +- Use least-privilege OAuth scopes.
    159 +- Store secrets in the approved vault, not repo files.
    160 +- Keep account recovery human-controlled.
    161 +- Do not give the agent browser-stored passwords in an unmanaged profile.
    162 +- Keep audit trail: every sent message, account created, and application submitted
    163 +  must link to a ClickUp task.
    164 +

     12 +# Fundraising Agentic Team Operating Model
     13 +
     14 +GTCX needs an agentic fundraising team that can operate across Africa/Global
     15 +South capital, pilots, partnerships, venture, revenue, startup resources, events,
     16 +applications, and ecosystem exposure.
     17 +
     18 +The team is not one general-purpose agent. It is a coordinated operating system
     19 +of specialized agents with clear responsibilities, shared memory, ClickUp
     20 +execution, email/calendar integration, and human approval gates.
     21 +
     22 +## Team Shape
     23 +
     24 +```text
     25 +Chief of Staff Agent
     26 +  -> Source Intelligence Agent
     27 +  -> Opportunity Analyst Agent
     28 +  -> CRM / ClickUp Operations Agent
     29 +  -> Inbox + Calendar Agent
     30 +  -> Outreach Drafting Agent
     31 +  -> Application + Proposal Agent
     32 +  -> Diligence + Data Room Agent
     33 +  -> Partnership / Sovereign Desk Agent
     34 +  -> Revenue / Buyer Desk Agent
     35 +  -> Investor Relations Agent
     36 +  -> Claims + Compliance Reviewer Agent
     37 +```
     38 +
     39 +## Team Roles
     40 +
     41 +| Agent | Mission | Primary Systems | Core Outputs |
     42 +| ----- | ------- | --------------- | ------------ |
     43 +| Chief of Staff Agent | Orchestrate the fundraising machine and keep priorities coherent | ClickUp, Gmail, Calendar,
          Drive | Daily brief, weekly agenda, escalation list |
     44 +| Source Intelligence Agent | Monitor sources, search queries, events, programs, and organization watchlists | Source
          DB, n8n, web listeners, ClickUp | New source signals, source freshness, daily source sweep |
     45 +| Opportunity Analyst Agent | Convert raw signals into qualified GTCX opportunities | Source DB, opportunity map, lea
         d schema | Fit score, product match, routing note, next action |
     46 +| CRM / ClickUp Operations Agent | Keep ClickUp clean, deduped, owner-assigned, and stage-accurate | ClickUp | Task c
         reation, field hygiene, stale-task cleanup |
     47 +| Inbox + Calendar Agent | Manage `hello@gtcx.trade`, aliases, labels, meeting flows, reminders | Gmail, Calendar, Cl
         ickUp | Thread summaries, draft-ready labels, meeting prep tasks |
     48 +| Outreach Drafting Agent | Draft high-quality external messages matched to audience and proof | Gmail drafts, decks,
          templates | Outreach drafts, follow-up drafts, intro notes |
     49 +| Application + Proposal Agent | Prepare applications, challenge submissions, grant packs, and pilot proposals | Clic
         kUp, Drive, docs, source materials | Submission plan, answer drafts, attachments checklist |
     50 +| Diligence + Data Room Agent | Maintain investor/partner diligence materials and evidence links | Drive, ClickUp, re
         po docs | Data room index, diligence tracker, evidence checklist |

    12 +# Fundraising Agent Authority Matrix
    13 +
    14 +Agents can accelerate the fundraising office, but they cannot become the legal,
    15 +financial, or relationship authority for GTCX.
    16 +
    17 +## Authority Matrix
    18 +
    19 +| Action | Agent Allowed | Human Approval | Notes |
    20 +| ------ | ------------- | -------------- | ----- |
    21 +| Read labeled `hello@gtcx.trade` mail | Yes | No | Only approved labels/aliases |
    22 +| Summarize email thread | Yes | No | Store summary, not full private body |
    23 +| Create ClickUp task | Yes | No | Must include source link and next action |
    24 +| Update ClickUp fields | Yes | No | Internal execution only |
    25 +| Move task to `Qualified` | Yes | No | If required fields and score are present |
    26 +| Move task to `Lost / Parked` | Yes | No | Must include reason |
    27 +| Draft outbound email | Yes | No | Draft only |
    28 +| Send outbound email | No | Yes | Unless later covered by approved narrow template |
    29 +| Reply to investor/DFI/bank/buyer | Draft only | Yes | Human relationship control |
    30 +| Schedule meeting | Propose only | Yes | Human approves external meeting |
    31 +| Create calendar hold | Yes | No | Internal hold only |
    32 +| Accept external calendar invite | No | Yes | External commitment |
    33 +| Create portal account | Prepare only | Yes | Human controls MFA/recovery |
    34 +| Accept terms | No | Yes | Legal gate |
    35 +| Submit application | No | Yes | External representation |
    36 +| Enter billing/payment info | No | Yes | Financial gate |
    37 +| Share deck/data room | No | Yes | Confidentiality gate |
    38 +| Make legal/financial claim | No | Yes | Claims reviewer must inspect first |
    39 +| Update source database | Yes | No | Internal intelligence |
    40 +| Add organization to watchlist | Yes | No | Internal intelligence |
    41 +| Recommend pursue/park | Yes | No | Recommendation only |
    42 +
    43 +## Escalation Triggers
    44 +
    45 +Escalate immediately when an item involves:
    46 +
    47 +1. Legal terms.
    48 +2. Payment, billing, or bank details.
    49 +3. Equity, SAFE, debt, revenue share, exclusivity, or IP terms.
    50 +4. Government, regulator, DFI, bank, investor, buyer, or press relationship.
    51 +5. Confidential decks, data rooms, financial models, or private source material.
    52 +6. Claims about production readiness, signed mandates, regulatory status, or revenue.
    53 +7. Any uncertainty about whether an external action binds GTCX.
    54 +
    55 +## Approval Record
    56 +
    57 +Every external action should leave an approval record in ClickUp:
    58 +
    59 +```text
    60 +Approved by:
    61 +Approved action:
    62 +Date/time:
    63 +Related draft/link:
    64 +Material risks:
    65 +Follow-up owner:
    66 +```

    12 +# Call Scripts
    13 +
    14 +## 15-Minute Discovery Call
    15 +
    16 +1. Context: "GTCX is building trust infrastructure for African commodity markets."
    17 +2. Their role: "How does your team touch {trade/finance/compliance/commodities}?"
    18 +3. Pain: "Where does verification, provenance, compliance, or buyer confidence slow things down?"
    19 +4. Current workflow: "How do you handle this today?"
    20 +5. Fit: "The possible overlap is {pilot/partnership/revenue/capital}."
    21 +6. Next step: "Would it be useful to send {brief/proposal/demo agenda}?"
    22 +
    23 +## Investor Call
    24 +
    25 +1. Thesis: African commodity markets lose value because legitimate producers cannot prove trust at market speed.
    26 +2. Product: GTCX creates reusable trust infrastructure across identity, location, compliance, custody, and transaction
         readiness.
    27 +3. Wedge: {country_or_corridor} + {commodity_or_partner}.
    28 +4. Business model: pilots, verification services, transaction fees, advisory, data/infrastructure products.
    29 +5. Ask: {amount_or_intro_or_design_partner}.
    30 +6. Proof needed: "The next milestone is {milestone}."
    31 +7. Close: "What would you need to see to take the next step?"
    32 +
    33 +## DFI / Sovereign / Trade Body Call
    34 +
    35 +1. Public-interest frame: formalization, market access, compliance evidence, trade facilitation.
    36 +2. Country/corridor: {country_or_corridor}.
    37 +3. Stakeholders: producers, buyers, regulators, banks, DFIs.
    38 +4. Pilot shape: small, measurable, evidence-producing.
    39 +5. Governance: data sovereignty, human approval, no regulatory overclaiming.
    40 +6. Next step: technical scoping or stakeholder-mapping session.
    41 +
    42 +## Buyer / Refiner / Trader Call
    43 +
    44 +1. Buyer problem: trust, provenance, compliance, and supplier risk.
    45 +2. Current workflow: what documents/evidence they require now.
    46 +3. Gap: what they still cannot verify.
    47 +4. GTCX fit: identity, GeoTag, GCI/compliance evidence, custody, transaction readiness.
    48 +5. Commercial path: pilot, API/integration, verification service, sourcing partnership.
    49 +6. Next step: compare one real workflow or sample transaction.

    12 +# Fundraising Engine Product Specification
    13 +
    14 +## Product
    15 +
    16 +The GTCX Fundraising Engine is an agentic operating system for finding,
    17 +qualifying, pursuing, and closing capital, partnerships, pilots, revenue deals,
    18 +startup resources, and ecosystem exposure across Africa and the Global South.
    19 +
    20 +## Problem
    21 +
    22 +GTCX has a large opportunity surface:
    23 +
    24 +- Africa-focused venture and catalytic capital.
    25 +- DFIs, MDBs, trade bodies, and public-sector pilots.
    26 +- Commodity buyers, refiners, traders, vaults, and banks.
    27 +- Accelerators, events, challenges, and startup-resource programs.
    28 +- Country-by-country ministries, regulators, producer networks, and ecosystem partners.
    29 +
    30 +Without a system, opportunities are lost in LinkedIn posts, emails, event pages,
    31 +funding windows, warm intros, and scattered docs. ClickUp alone cannot hold the
    32 +source graph, and a general-purpose assistant cannot run the workflow reliably.
    33 +
    34 +## Users
    35 +
    36 +| User | Job |
    37 +| ---- | --- |
    38 +| Founder / executive | Decide priorities, approve external moves, lead strategic relationships |
    39 +| Fundraising operator | Run daily pipeline, inbox, follow-ups, applications, and reporting |
    40 +| Agentic team | Monitor, analyze, draft, prepare, review, and maintain systems |
    41 +| Product leads | Supply product proof, collateral, pilot scopes, and diligence responses |
    42 +| Advisors / warm intro network | Provide introductions, review decks, validate strategy |
    43 +
    44 +## Product Thesis
    45 +
    46 +Build the intelligence layer separately from the execution layer:
    47 +
    48 +| Layer | System |
    49 +| ----- | ------ |
    50 +| Intelligence | Source database, organization graph, opportunity map, search queries, scoring, dedupe |
    51 +| Execution | ClickUp tasks, owners, statuses, follow-ups, collateral requests, approvals |
    52 +| Communication | `hello@gtcx.trade`, aliases, Gmail, Calendar, WhatsApp, LinkedIn manual capture |
    53 +| Automation | n8n, RSS, page watch, email labels, calendar sync |
    54 +| Future app | Postgres-backed fundraising intelligence app with ClickUp sync |
    55 +
    56 +## In Scope
    57 +
    58 +1. Source intelligence and recurring web/search/email monitoring.
    59 +2. ClickUp CRM and pipeline setup.
    60 +3. Agentic team workflows and authority model.
    61 +4. Email/calendar operations through `hello@gtcx.trade`.
    62 +5. Collateral, concept notes, pitch decks, and data-room workflows.
    63 +6. Application, challenge, proposal, and program submission workflows.
    64 +7. Finance/deal economics review workflows.
    65 +8. Daily and weekly operating cadence.
    66 +9. Data model and integration specs for future app build.
    67 +
    68 +## Out Of Scope For V1
    69 +
    70 +1. Automated LinkedIn scraping.
    71 +2. Autonomous external sending.
    72 +3. Autonomous application submissions.
    73 +4. Autonomous legal/financial commitments.
    74 +5. Paid CRM replacement.
    75 +6. Public-facing investor portal.
    76 +
    77 +## Key Capabilities
    78 +
    79 +| Capability | V1 | V2 |
    80 +| ---------- | -- | -- |

    82 +| Search query library | Manual/n8n runs | Scheduled search ingestion |

    87 +| Collateral workflow | ClickUp tasks + templates | Asset library with versioning |

    11 +
    12 +# Fundraising Engine Requirements
    13 +
    14 +## Objective
    15 +
    16 +Build a fundraising operating system that lets GTCX continuously discover,
    17 +qualify, pursue, and close Africa/Global South capital, pilots, partnerships,
    18 +revenue opportunities, startup programs, and ecosystem exposure with auditable
    19 +human control.
    20 +
    21 +## Functional Requirements
    22 +
    23 +| ID | Requirement | V1 System Of Record | Acceptance |
    24 +| -- | ----------- | ------------------- | ---------- |
    25 +| FR1 | Maintain source database for monitored web links, organizations, events, programs, and queries | `fundraising/
        sources/` | Every source has owner, listener mode, category, geography, and URL |
    26 +| FR2 | Convert source signals into qualified opportunities | ClickUp + source docs | Opportunity has source URL, fit
        score, class, owner, status, and next action |
    27 +| FR3 | Track CRM pipeline and status | ClickUp | No active opportunity lacks owner, deadline, status, or next action
        |
    28 +| FR4 | Manage fundraising email and calendar | Gmail/Calendar + ClickUp | Every relevant thread/meeting maps to a tas
        k or contact record |
    29 +| FR5 | Produce collateral and deliverables | Drive + ClickUp | Each asset has audience, version, status, claims revie
        w, and approval state |
    30 +| FR6 | Route finance and deal economics review | ClickUp + financial model docs | All terms, budgets, pricing, runway
        , dilution, and revenue-share claims are reviewed |
    31 +| FR7 | Support applications, proposals, and challenge submissions | ClickUp + Drive | Submission checklist, deadline,
         attachments, approval, and confirmation are linked |
    32 +| FR8 | Publish daily fundraising brief | ClickUp/n8n/manual digest | Brief includes top opportunities, replies, meeti
        ngs, deadlines, blockers, and approvals |
    33 +| FR9 | Maintain agentic team handoffs | Team docs + ClickUp | Handoff includes context, requested output, due date, s
        ource links, and approval need |
    34 +| FR10 | Preserve human approval for external actions | ClickUp custom field | External sends, submissions, commitment
        s, and sensitive claims require approval |
    35 +
    36 +## Data Requirements
    37 +
    38 +| Entity | Required Fields |
    39 +| ------ | --------------- |
    40 +| Source | ID, name, URL, category, geography, listener mode, priority, owner, review cadence |
    41 +| Organization | Name, type, geography, relevance, relationship status, contacts, source links |
    42 +| Signal | Source, URL, excerpt, detected date, deadline, organization, tags, dedupe key |
    43 +| Opportunity | Title, type, class, stage, fit score, amount/value, deadline, owner, next action |
    44 +| Contact | Name, organization, role, channel, relationship path, consent/context, last touch |
    45 +| Deliverable | Type, audience, opportunity, status, version, owner, reviewers, final link |
    46 +| Approval | Action, requester, approver, approval state, timestamp, notes, evidence link |
    47 +
    48 +## Integration Requirements
    49 +
    50 +| Integration | Requirement | Constraint |
    51 +| ----------- | ----------- | ---------- |
    52 +| ClickUp | Primary CRM, task workflow, status tracking, dashboards | Must work manually before app sync |
    53 +| Gmail | Managed inbox for `hello@gtcx.trade` and aliases | Agents draft; humans approve sends |
    54 +| Calendar | Meeting prep, agenda, follow-up, deadline management | Meetings must link to task/opportunity |
    55 +| Drive | Collateral, data room, submissions, evidence | Assets must use controlled folders and version labels |
    56 +| n8n | Low-code automation for inbox, calendar, digest, listeners | Automation cannot bypass approval gates |
    57 +| LinkedIn | Manual capture, outreach drafting, relationship research | No automated scraping or sending in V1 |
    58 +| Web sources | RSS, page watch, search sweeps, manual review | Must save source URL and excerpt |
    59 +| Future app | Intelligence database and dashboard | ClickUp remains execution system until migration decision |
    60 +
    61 +## Control Requirements
    62 +
    63 +| Control | Rule |
    64 +| ------- | ---- |
    65 +| External communications | No send without human approval |
    66 +| LinkedIn activity | No automated scraping, posting, connection requests, or messaging in V1 |
    67 +| Applications | No submission without eligibility confirmation and approval |
    68 +| Finance claims | No budget, valuation, pricing, ROI, dilution, or revenue-share claim without finance review |
    69 +| Legal/commercial terms | No signature, commitment, exclusivity, or binding term without human/legal review |
    70 +| Claims | No market, regulatory, impact, ESG, traceability, or product claim without evidence link |
    71 +| Credentials | Agents do not hold owner credentials unless explicitly delegated through approved systems |
    72 +| Data retention | Keep source, communication, approval, and submission evidence linked to the opportunity |
    73 +
    74 +## Non-Functional Requirements
    75 +
    76 +| Area | Requirement |
    77 +| ---- | ----------- |
    78 +| Auditability | Every opportunity should be reconstructable from source signal to decision |
    79 +| Speed | Daily source signals should be triaged within one business day |
    80 +| Coverage | System must scale toward all African countries and major Global South capital corridors |
    81 +| Dedupe | Duplicate opportunities must merge or link across source, email, LinkedIn, and ClickUp |
    82 +| Portability | Core source and workflow data must remain exportable outside any paid SaaS |
    83 +| Cost | V1 uses existing/free/open-source tooling wherever possible |
    84 +| Security | Access to inbox, calendar, and Drive must follow least privilege |
    85 +| Reliability | Manual fallback must exist for every automated workflow |
    86 +
    87 +## MVP Definition
    88 +
    89 +The MVP is complete when GTCX can run a full fundraising week with:
    90 +
    91 +1. A live ClickUp fundraising CRM.
    92 +2. `hello@gtcx.trade` labels and aliases mapped to workflows.
    93 +3. Daily source review across the seed database, watchlist, and query library.
    94 +4. At least 50 processed signals and 10 qualified opportunities.
    95 +5. At least 5 approved outreach drafts.
    96 +6. Meeting prep and follow-up tasks created for all fundraising meetings.
    97 +7. At least 2 collateral assets drafted, reviewed, and linked.
    98 +8. Zero external actions without recorded approval.

• Yes. This can become a real product, but the right path is internal operating system first, external product second.
  The product is not “fundraising software” in the generic CRM sense. The defensible product is:
  GTCX Capital Intelligence OS
  An AI-assisted operating system for frontier-market companies, accelerators, funds, DFIs, and ecosystem builders to
  discover, qualify, pursue, and manage capital, pilots, partnerships, grants, revenue deals, events, and strategic exposure.
  The wedge is Africa / Global South opportunity intelligence.
  Why This Could Be A Product
  Most founders and ecosystem operators lose opportunities because funding intelligence is fragmented across:
  - LinkedIn posts
  - donor portals
  - DFI/MDB sites
  - accelerator pages
  - WhatsApp groups
  - newsletters
  - event sites
  - government/procurement portals
  - VC announcements
  - warm-intro networks
  - Gmail/calendar chaos
  Existing tools solve pieces: CRM, grant search, investor database, task management, inbox automation. Very few solve the
  operating workflow end-to-end for frontier-market fundraising.
  Product Shape
  The product could have three layers:
  1. Intelligence Layer
      - Source database
      - Search listeners
      - LinkedIn/manual capture
      - Funding/program/event watchlists
      - Organization graph
      - Opportunity scoring
      - Country and sector maps
  2. Execution Layer
      - Pipeline CRM
      - Status tracking
      - Task owners
      - Deadlines
      - Outreach workflows
      - Application workflows
      - Meeting prep/follow-up
      - ClickUp/Gmail/Calendar integration
  3. Agentic Team Layer
      - Source scout agent
      - Opportunity analyst agent
      - Outreach drafting agent
      - Proposal/application agent
      - Finance/deal terms agent
      - Collateral/deck agent
      - Compliance/claims reviewer
      - Chief of staff agent
  That is much stronger than “AI grant finder.”
  Best Initial Product Packaging
  I would package it in this order:
  1. Internal GTCX Fundraising Engine
     Build and prove it against our own pipeline.
  2. Managed Service
     “We run your Africa/Global South capital pipeline for you.”
     This is fastest to revenue because the product can be semi-manual behind the scenes.
  3. Private Beta App
     For founders, accelerators, ecosystem hubs, and funds.
  4. SaaS / Intelligence Platform
     Subscription product with source monitoring, CRM, agents, templates, and workflows.
  Likely Customers
  Strong early buyers/users:
  - African startups raising capital
  - accelerators and incubators
  - diaspora founder networks
  - venture studios
  - ecosystem hubs
  - DFIs and foundations tracking portfolio opportunities
  - trade associations
  - commodity/fintech/climate/agritech startups
  - universities and innovation centers
  - government innovation agencies
  Revenue Models
  Possible pricing:
  - Managed service: $1,500-$10,000/month depending on scope
  - Founder SaaS: $49-$299/month
  - Accelerator license: $2,000-$20,000/program
  - Enterprise ecosystem license: $25k-$250k/year
  - Success fee: only carefully, depending on legal/regulatory context
  - Data/API product: paid access to Africa/Global South opportunity intelligence
  - White-label version: for incubators, banks, funds, and ecosystem organizations
  The Product Wedge
  The strongest wedge is:
  > “The capital, grants, pilot, and partnership intelligence layer for Africa and Global South ventures.”
  Not generic fundraising. Not another CRM. Not another AI assistant.
  The moat is the source graph, workflows, templates, scoring, and accumulated opportunity data.
  What We Should Build First
  The first product MVP should be:
  - Source database
  - Opportunity database
  - ClickUp sync
  - Gmail/Calendar integration
  - Daily digest
  - Opportunity scoring
  - Manual LinkedIn capture workflow
  - Agent-generated outreach drafts
  - Application/proposal tracker
  - Collateral request workflow
  - Approval/audit log
  The first sellable version can be service-led, with the app acting as the internal command center.
  My Recommendation
  Yes, productize it, but do not start as a polished SaaS. Start as:
  Capital Desk by GTCX
  A managed AI-powered capital intelligence and fundraising operations desk for Africa/Global South companies and ecosystem
  partners.
  Then turn repeated workflows into software.
  That gives GTCX two benefits at once: it helps us fund the company, and it becomes a revenue-producing product line.

     11 +
     12 +# Venture OS Modules and Workflows
     13 +
     14 +## Operating Loop
     15 +
     16 +Venture OS runs a weekly founder-growth loop:
     17 +
     18 +1. Monitor sources.
     19 +2. Capture signals.
     20 +3. Score opportunities.
     21 +4. Route to capital, revenue, partner, visibility, or collateral workflow.
     22 +5. Draft and prepare.
     23 +6. Review claims, finance, and approvals.
     24 +7. Execute human-approved outreach/submission.
     25 +8. Follow up and update pipeline.
     26 +9. Publish weekly founder brief.
     27 +
     28 +## W1: Source Signal To Opportunity
     29 +
     30 +Trigger:
     31 +
     32 +- Web listener finds a new opportunity.
     33 +- LinkedIn/manual capture adds a signal.
     34 +- Newsletter/email creates a lead.
     35 +- Partner/advisor shares a warm intro.
     36 +
     37 +Flow:
     38 +
     39 +1. Create signal with URL, excerpt, date, organization, geography, source type.
     40 +2. Dedupe against existing signals/opportunities.
     41 +3. Score by strategic fit, timing, credibility, expected value, and effort.
     42 +4. Route to opportunity stage or research queue.
     43 +5. Assign owner and next action.
     44 +
     45 +Acceptance:
     46 +
     47 +- Signal has source URL and excerpt.
     48 +- Opportunity has type, score, owner, deadline, status, and next action.
     49 +
     50 +## W2: Capital Opportunity Workflow
     51 +
     52 +Applies to VC, grants, foundations, DFIs, accelerators, challenges, angels, and
     53 +catalytic capital.
     54 +
     55 +Flow:
     56 +
     57 +1. Confirm eligibility and strategic fit.
     58 +2. Identify funding amount, instrument, deadline, decision process.
     59 +3. Map required collateral.
     60 +4. Prepare outreach or application.
     61 +5. Review claims and finance.
     62 +6. Founder approves.
     63 +7. Submit/send.
     64 +8. Save evidence and schedule follow-up.
     65 +
     66 +Deliverables:
     67 +
     68 +- Investor brief.
     69 +- Application checklist.
     70 +- Pitch deck / one-pager.
     71 +- Financial model extract.
     72 +- Follow-up sequence.
     73 +
     74 +## W3: Revenue And Pilot Workflow
     75 +
     76 +Applies to paid pilots, buyer relationships, enterprise procurement, channels,
     77 +and anchor customers.
     78 +
     79 +Flow:
     80 +
     81 +1. Define buyer problem and product fit.
     82 +2. Identify economic buyer, user, influencer, procurement path.
     83 +3. Draft pilot scope and success criteria.
     84 +4. Create commercial ask.
     85 +5. Review pricing and terms.
     86 +6. Founder approves outreach/proposal.
     87 +7. Track meeting, proposal, negotiation, close.
     88 +
     89 +Deliverables:
     90 +
     91 +- Buyer brief.
     92 +- Pilot concept note.
     93 +- Commercial proposal.
     94 +- Pricing/options memo.
     95 +- Meeting prep and follow-up.
     96 +
     97 +## W4: Partnership Workflow
     98 +
     99 +Applies to DFIs, sovereigns, banks, telcos, universities, NGOs, standards bodies,
    100 +trade associations, and corporate programs.
    101 +
    102 +Flow:
    103 +
    104 +1. Classify partner type and strategic value.
    105 +2. Map relationship path and decision structure.
    106 +3. Define partnership thesis and mutual value.
    107 +4. Draft concept note or intro.
    108 +5. Review policy/regulatory/claims risk.
    109 +6. Founder approves.
    110 +7. Track meeting, MoU, pilot, sponsorship, or joint program.

    112 +Deliverables:
    113 +
    114 +- Partner map.
    115 +- Partnership thesis.
    116 +- Concept note.
    117 +- MoU term sheet draft.
    118 +- Stakeholder briefing.
    119 +
    120 +## W5: Visibility Workflow
    121 +
    122 +Applies to LinkedIn, press, podcasts, speaking, awards, events, newsletters, and
    123 +ecosystem exposure.
    124 +
    125 +Flow:
    126 +
    127 +1. Capture visibility opportunity or story signal.

     12 +# Venture OS Product Specification
     13 +
     14 +## Product
     15 +
     16 +Venture OS is an AI-assisted operating system for African and Global South
     17 +ventures to raise capital, grow revenue, build visibility, manage investor and
     18 +partner relationships, produce high-quality collateral, and scale execution.
     19 +
     20 +## Wedge
     21 +
     22 +The wedge is not generic startup CRM. The wedge is:
     23 +
     24 +> The venture growth office for Africa-focused companies operating across
     25 +> capital, pilots, partnerships, revenue, visibility, and proof.
     26 +
     27 +## Users
     28 +
     29 +| User | Job |
     30 +| ---- | --- |
     31 +| Founder / CEO | Decide priorities, approve relationship moves, pitch, close capital and deals |
     32 +| Fundraising lead | Manage pipeline, deadlines, outreach, investor updates, applications |
     33 +| Growth lead | Turn market signals into revenue, partnerships, distribution, campaigns |
     34 +| Venture studio / accelerator operator | Run repeatable growth/fundraising workflows across portfolio companies |
     35 +| Fund / ecosystem builder | Track companies, opportunities, milestones, visibility, introductions |
     36 +| Agentic team | Monitor, draft, score, prep, route, review, and maintain systems |
     37 +
     38 +## Customer Segments
     39 +
     40 +| Segment | Pain | Product Promise |
     41 +| ------- | ---- | --------------- |
     42 +| African startups | Opportunity flow is fragmented and founder time is scarce | Daily capital/growth operating syste
         m |
     43 +| Venture studios | Portfolio companies need repeatable fundraising and growth support | Shared venture-growth back o
         ffice |
     44 +| Accelerators/incubators | Program teams need better funding outcomes and visibility for cohorts | Cohort capital an
         d visibility engine |
     45 +| Funds/foundations | Portfolio support is manual and inconsistent | Scalable founder success desk |
     46 +| Ecosystem hubs | Need to connect founders to capital, partners, press, and programs | Regional opportunity intellig
         ence layer |
     47 +| Corporate/DFI programs | Need pipeline visibility and credible venture engagement | Qualified venture pipeline and
         proof layer |
     48 +
     49 +## Modules
     50 +
     51 +| Module | Description |
     52 +| ------ | ----------- |
     53 +| Capital Desk | Grants, VC, angels, DFIs, foundations, accelerators, challenges, non-dilutive capital |
     54 +| Revenue Desk | Buyers, paid pilots, enterprise sales, procurement, channel deals |
     55 +| Partnership Desk | DFIs, sovereigns, banks, telcos, universities, corporates, NGOs, ecosystem partners |
     56 +| Investor Relations | CRM, warm intros, updates, diligence, meeting prep, follow-up |
     57 +| Visibility Desk | LinkedIn, press, events, awards, newsletters, thought leadership |
     58 +| Collateral Factory | Pitch decks, one-pagers, concept notes, proposals, case studies, data rooms |
     59 +| Founder Command Center | Daily priorities, approvals, blockers, deadlines, calendar, inbox |
     60 +| Proof Desk | Claims, metrics, traction evidence, compliance, audit trail, impact proof |
     61 +| Growth Intelligence | Source database, web listeners, search sweeps, event/program tracker |
     62 +| Client Success | Onboarding, maturity scoring, weekly operating rhythm, progress reports |
     63 +
     64 +## MVP
     65 +
     66 +V1 should be service-led and app-assisted:
     67 +
     68 +1. ClickUp workspace template.
     69 +2. Gmail/Calendar/Drive operating model.
     70 +3. Source database and search query library.
     71 +4. Multi-client opportunity CRM.
     72 +5. Daily digest.
     73 +6. Weekly founder brief.
     74 +7. Outreach and collateral template library.
     75 +8. Agentic team playbooks.
     76 +9. Approval and claims controls.
     77 +10. Lightweight client profile schema.
     78 +
     79 +## Future App
     80 +
     81 +The app should become the intelligence layer, not merely another task manager.
     82 +
     83 +| App Area | Purpose |
     84 +| -------- | ------- |
     85 +| Clients | Company profiles, maturity, positioning, product portfolio, goals |
     86 +| Opportunities | Capital, revenue, partnerships, events, visibility, programs |
     87 +| Source Graph | Web links, search queries, organizations, people, newsletters |
     88 +| Relationship Graph | Investors, partners, buyers, press, advisors, warm intro paths |
     89 +| Deliverables | Decks, one-pagers, proposals, applications, updates, press assets |
     90 +| Digest | Daily/weekly command center across inbox, calendar, sources, ClickUp |
     91 +| Approvals | External sends, claims, submissions, financial/legal commitments |
     92 +| Analytics | Pipeline health, conversion, velocity, visibility, funding outcomes |
     93 +
     94 +## Differentiation
     95 +
     96 +| Generic Tool | Venture OS Difference |
     97 +| ------------ | --------------------- |
     98 +| CRM | Built for capital, pilots, partnerships, visibility, and founder execution |
     99 +| Grant database | Includes revenue deals, investor relationships, press, events, and partnerships |
    100 +| AI writing tool | Has workflows, approvals, proof, source intelligence, and human control |
    101 +| Accelerator portal | Can operate across cohorts, warm intros, applications, and visibility |
    102 +| Investor database | Tracks relationship paths, collateral, diligence, and follow-up execution |
    103 +
    104 +## V1 Control Rules
    105 +
    106 +1. No autonomous external sends.
    107 +2. No autonomous LinkedIn scraping or messaging.
    108 +3. No application submissions without human approval.
    109 +4. No public claims without evidence.
    110 +5. No financial terms, pricing, valuation, dilution, or revenue-share claims
    111 +   without finance review.
    112 +6. No legal commitments without founder/legal approval.
    113 +7. Every active opportunity must have owner, source, status, and next action.
